module-3-clean-release-snapshot.label: Project: Prepare a Clean Release Snapshot
module-3-clean-release-snapshot.summary: A volunteer toolkit needs a careful file cleanup before handoff. Rename, remove, ignore, restore, commit, and verify without rewriting history.

try_module_3_sketch5.title: Commit and Verify the Release Cleanup

try_module_3_sketch0.title: Inspect the Release Candidate
try_module_3_sketch0.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Read short status, tracked files, and one-line history before changing the release candidate.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_3_sketch0.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: A safe cleanup begins by establishing the current tracked state and history.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_3_sketch0.solutionCode:
git status --short
git ls-files
git log --oneline
try_module_3_sketch0.instructions:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Read short status, tracked files, and one-line history before changing the release candidate.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-snapshot
mkdir -p release-snapshot
git -C release-snapshot init -q -b main
git -C release-snapshot config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-snapshot config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-snapshot
printf %s '# Volunteer Toolkit
' > README.md
cd ..
cd release-snapshot
mkdir -p docs
printf %s '# Draft Volunteer Guide
' > docs/guide-draft.md
cd ..
cd release-snapshot
mkdir -p docs
printf %s 'Old checklist
' > docs/old-checklist.md
cd ..
git -C release-snapshot add -A
git -C release-snapshot commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'

cd release-snapshot
printf %s '# Volunteer Toolkit
' > README.md
cd ..
cd release-snapshot
mkdir -p docs
printf %s '# Draft Volunteer Guide
' > docs/guide-draft.md
cd ..
cd release-snapshot
mkdir -p docs
printf %s 'Old checklist
' > docs/old-checklist.md
cd ..
git -C release-snapshot add -A
git -C release-snapshot commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'

0.message: Do not rewrite shared history in this Git practice task.
Run `git status --short`, then check your answer again.

1.message: Run `git ls-files`, then check your answer again.

2.message: Run `git log --oneline`, then check your answer again.

try_module_3_sketch1.title: Rename the Volunteer Guide
try_module_3_sketch1.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Rename `docs/guide-draft.md` to `docs/volunteer-guide.md` with Git and verify the staged structural change.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_3_sketch1.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The rename becomes the first staged part of the release cleanup.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_3_sketch1.solutionCode:
git mv docs/guide-draft.md docs/volunteer-guide.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_module_3_sketch1.instructions:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Rename `docs/guide-draft.md` to `docs/volunteer-guide.md` with Git and verify the staged structural change.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

try_module_3_sketch2.title: Remove the Obsolete Checklist
try_module_3_sketch2.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The guide rename is already staged. Remove `docs/old-checklist.md` with Git, then review both staged changes.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_3_sketch2.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The project cleanup accumulates without committing until the intended release boundary is complete.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.

try_module_3_sketch3.title: Ignore Preview Output
try_module_3_sketch3.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The rename and removal are staged, and `preview.log` is untracked. Add `*.log` to `.gitignore`, stage the policy, and verify the preview is ignored.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_3_sketch3.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The ignore policy belongs in history; the generated preview does not.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_3_sketch3.solutionCode:
printf "*.log\n" > .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git check-ignore preview.log
git diff --staged --name-only
try_module_3_sketch3.instructions:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The rename and removal are staged, and `preview.log` is untracked. Add `*.log` to `.gitignore`, stage the policy, and verify the preview is ignored.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

try_module_3_sketch4.title: Restore an Accidental README Edit
try_module_3_sketch4.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The cleanup is staged, but `README.md` has an accidental unstaged edit. Restore only README and verify the intended staged work remains.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_3_sketch4.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: A targeted restore removes an accidental working-tree edit without disturbing the staged cleanup.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_3_sketch4.instructions:

The cleanup is staged, but `README.md` has an accidental unstaged edit. Restore only README and verify the intended staged work remains.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

try_module_3_sketch5.title: Commit and Verify the Clean Release
try_module_3_sketch5.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Commit the completed cleanup as `Prepare clean volunteer release`, then read the history, tracked files, and clean status.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_3_sketch5.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The final verification should prove the release snapshot, history, and working tree all agree.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_3_sketch5.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Prepare clean volunteer release"
git log --oneline
git ls-files
git status --short
try_module_3_sketch5.instructions:

intro-sketch.title: Prepare a Repository for Handoff
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown:
A volunteer handbook contains a tracked draft name, an obsolete note, and generated preview output. Before the next maintainer takes over, the repository needs a clean, intentional shape.

You will review tracked changes, rename and remove paths with Git-aware commands, add a precise ignore rule, restore one mistake, and create a focused release snapshot.

The handoff is complete when history is readable, generated output stays untracked, and status is clean.
